author: nero_eth
content:
For today's BAL breakout as part of ACDT, I'd propose to use most of the time in the BAL breakout for 8037 discussions (cc <@968526635735326741>):

For the first part of the breakout (EIP-7928), that's the proposed agenda:
* Move BlockAccessIndex from `uint16` to `uint64`.
  * EIP update: https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11535
  * Specs update: https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/pull/2713
* Benchmarking updates 

Please lmk if there are any other agenda items to discuss.

author: draganrakita
content:
<@792404665068158998> i would like to discuss idea on how we can sort BAL items. The statements:
* Sorting them by first access makes sense as you can load state with priority.
* It is very complex to sort access of every sload/sstore, account load/write with reverts etc inside tx. We dont want this.
* We can still have benefit if we order tx batches.
* So proposal is, tx batch output gets sorted and appended in order to the BAL lists.
* Upside ordered load (great), downside additional verification vector. If client are recreating BAL for verification than downside is maybe not of high impact.

author: nero_eth
content: yes, good point that has also been brought up by other clients in the past, e.g. I remember <@628018527470616606> and <@316280621783580673> advocating for exploring this too.

author: nero_eth
content: What would you define as a tx batch here? Multiple txs?

author: draganrakita
content: i meant batch as state reads/writes inside one tx

author: nero_eth
content: I see, you we would not order by access within a tx, but by order of txs, and within a tx we keep it lexicographically ordered?

author: draganrakita
content: Yes, imo it is too complex being in consensus if we order them by access within a tx.

author: nero_eth
content: Yeah, let's discuss that today. I could see this being a good trade-off between adding gains with still minimized complexity.

author: nero_eth
content:
Would be great if you could summarize your idea briefly, how each item in the BAL would then be treated. E.g. storage reads would be ordered by actual access I assume? What about addresses inside the BAL?
E.g. a worst-case would be I access an address in the first tx, by doing a simple call do it and then only in the last tx I read many storage slots (the actual work) of that address. In that case, I would trick your sorting approach, no?
<@429719902363058177>
